mentions.note: Spun off Feb 2025, rallied 371% rest of year; data center memory demand insatiable for AI. Historically memory cycles boom-then-bust, but cautious supply expansion may extend runway. Cramer advises: trim positions, play with house money.
Storage supply shortage driving 28% gains; demand voracious and pricing up sharply. Short-sellers getting heads handed to them. Cramer suggests ringing register on part of position given volatility.
Memory stock more than tripled in 2025; now cooling. Recommend taking profits.
Data storage play moving up with semiconductor capital equipment companies.
Memory chip maker up 72% YTD due to severe shortage of data storage chips for AI data centers; customers desperate for supply.
Storage semiconductor manufacturer; caught fire due to data-center rage; stock up 74% in two weeks after 200%+ 2025 gain; supply shortage driving outsized gains.
Memory supplier up 111% YTD on extreme AI demand; parabolic move without peak; machinery bottleneck keeps shortage alive; buy pullback, avoid chasing.
Storage play up 112% YTD in remarkable rally; underinvested for years, caught flatfooted by AI explosion; now raising prices repeatedly with no resistance; galloping to where it has to go.
Storage company; up 122% YTD; top 10 performer in S&P500 last year; expect continued strength on storage shortage.
Memory device company benefiting from AI data center buildout; earnings and guidance beat sent stock soaring.
Blowout Q: earned $6.20 vs. $3.62 consensus; guiding $12–14 FY2026 EPS vs. $5–11 street. January's #1 performer, up 143% on data center AI demand. No supply visible yet.
Memory shortage plays with severe supply constraint; insatiable AI data demand; no demand destruction expected; memory prices can skyrocket with no ceiling in sight.
Memory king trading rapidly higher—primary signal Cramer uses to predict risk-on sessions. When memory stocks spike, semis and risk assets follow.
Memory stock mentioned alongside WDC, STX; too high but buy on big pullback if oil soars. Memory shortage theme intact.
Memory/storage stock rallying on data-center demand, but represents a tax on system via shortage-driven pricing. Cramer prefers high-IP content (fiber optics, networking) over commodity memory plays.
Stock ran 85% more after Cramer said miss it in January; rare staying power; Cramer's discipline of avoiding parabolic movers sometimes betrays him; long-term discipline still wins.
Memory stock up 8.4% today (+$75) on desperate buyers placing orders with high top prices; years-long shortage in AI-hungry data center demand.
Up 317% YTD alongside Western Digital. Memory shortage supports blowaway numbers; if they keep hitting, can go higher.
Memory and storage ramping; up 7% today. Essential server components in short supply alongside Seagate and Western Digital.
Memory/storage supplier gaining from data center buildout but stock massively parabolic; sell on strength—too extended for entry.
Trading under 10x FY2026 earnings despite a 3,500% 12-month gain; AI data center storage shortage has structurally transformed it into a growth stock with pricing power and still-rising estimates.
Up 4,162% over 12 months; real company with real demand; could be even cheaper than Micron despite bigger rally; pick and stick with it.
Up 3000% in 12 months; 20x forward earnings still may be too expensive vs. Micron. Let it come down more than today's 5%.
Invented memory cards; memory company crushing it on AI/data infrastructure demand; real profits, not dot-com story.
